2026-03-10.title: West Bengal SIR test: reading the Supreme Court’s order
2026-03-10.title_hi: पश्चिम बंगाल में चुनावी मतदाता सूची विवाद: सुप्रीम कोर्ट के आदेश की व्याख्या
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Election Commission and Electoral Reforms
2026-03-10.context: The Supreme Court's order comes amidst 60 lakh pending cases in West Bengal. The Election Commission's ability to handle electoral roll disputes has been questioned. The publication of final rolls has significant implications for the state's electoral process. The Supreme Court's decision aims to address the backlog of cases. The Election Commission's staffing has been a topic of discussion, with some arguing for permanent staff to handle such disputes.
key_points: The Supreme Court — Electoral Roll Disputes: Ordered deployment of judicial officers to decide cases.
Election Commission — Staffing: Debate triggered on equipping with permanent staff to handle disputes.
West Bengal Government — Electoral Process: Publication of final rolls has significant implications.
The Supreme Court — Judicial Officers: Aims to address backlog of 60 lakh pending cases.
Election Commission — Electoral Roll Disputes: Handling of disputes has been questioned due to large number of pending cases.

2026-03-10.title: Prime Minister to launch projects worth ₹10,800 crore at Kochi event
2026-03-10.title_hi: प्रधानमंत्री ₹10,800 करोड़ की परियोजनाओं का शुभारंभ करेंगे
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Infrastructure Development
2026-03-10.context: The Prime Minister will lay the foundation stone for a polypropylene unit at the Kochi Refinery, as part of the projects worth ₹10,800 crore. The event will also see the inauguration of 23 rural roads constructed under the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana in the State. The projects are expected to boost infrastructure development in the region. The Kochi Refinery is a major oil refinery in the country, and the addition of a polypropylene unit is expected to increase its production capacity. The Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana is a flagship scheme of the government aimed at improving rural connectivity.
key_points: The Prime Minister — Kochi Refinery: Laying foundation stone for a polypropylene unit (₹10,800 crore).
The Prime Minister — Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana: Inaugurating 23 rural roads.
The Government — Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana: Aiming to improve rural connectivity.
The Kochi Refinery — Polypropylene Unit: Expected to increase production capacity.
The Government — Infrastructure Development: Boosting development in the region through these projects.

2026-03-10.title: Supreme Court approves Centre's proposal extending term of tribunal members
2026-03-10.title_hi: उच्चतम न्यायालय ने केंद्र के प्रस्ताव को मंजूरी दी, ट्रिब्यूनल सदस्यों की अवधि बढ़ाई
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Judiciary and Tribunal Reforms
2026-03-10.context: The Supreme Court has approved the Centre's proposal to extend the term of tribunal members appointed under the Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021. About 21 members are due to retire in the meantime. A new law is likely to be enacted by next September, either in this Budget session or the Monsoon session. The Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021 will continue to apply to everybody who was appointed under it. The Attorney General R Venkataramani stated that the new law will be enacted soon.
key_points: Supreme Court — Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021: Approved extension of term for tribunal members.
Centre — Proposal: Extended term for about 21 members due to retire.
Attorney General R Venkataramani — Statement: New law likely to be enacted by next September.
Government — Budget session/Monsoon session: New law expected to be passed.
Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021 — Provision: Continues to apply to everybody appointed under it.

2026-03-10.title: We will have to approach court against Centre to seek justice on devolution of funds, says CM Siddaramaiah
2026-03-10.title_hi: केंद्र से फंड के बंटवारे पर न्याय की मांग के लिए अदालत का दरवाजा खटखटाना पड़ेगा: सिद्धारमैया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Interstate Relations and Centre-State Relations
2026-03-10.context: The Karnataka Chief Minister, Siddaramaiah, has stated that the state may have to approach the court to seek justice on the devolution of funds from the Centre. Karnataka had earlier managed to get drought relief assistance of about ₹3,000 crore from the Centre after it had approached the Supreme Court. This move is seen as a last resort to resolve the issue. The state government has been seeking a higher share of funds from the Centre. The Centre's decision on fund allocation has been a point of contention between the state and the Centre.
key_points: The Karnataka government — Drought Relief Assistance: Received ₹3,000 crore from the Centre after approaching the Supreme Court.
The Karnataka government — Fund Devolution: Seeking a higher share of funds from the Centre.
The Centre — Fund Allocation: Decision has been a point of contention between the state and the Centre.
The Supreme Court — Drought Relief Assistance: Provided relief to Karnataka in the past.
The Karnataka government — Court Approach: May approach the court to seek justice on fund devolution.

2026-03-10.title: UP govt signs pact with Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity
2026-03-10.title_hi: उत्तर प्रदेश सरकार ने हिंदुजा समूह की कंपनी के साथ ब्रॉडबैंड कनेक्टिविटी विस्तार के लिए समझौता किया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-10.context: The Uttar Pradesh government has signed a pact with a Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity in the state. The pact aims to provide high-speed internet services to over 30,000 gram panchayats in the state. The project is expected to be completed within the next 12 months at a cost of ₹2,500 crore. The Hinduja Group firm will be responsible for laying down the necessary infrastructure, including fiber optic cables and mobile towers. The project is expected to benefit over 10 million people in the state.
key_points: Uttar Pradesh government — Broadband Expansion Project: Signed pact with Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity to 30,000 gram panchayats.
Hinduja Group firm — Broadband Expansion Project: Will lay down fiber optic cables and mobile towers at a cost of ₹2,500 crore.
Uttar Pradesh government — BharatNet Initiative: Aims to provide high-speed internet services to over 10 million people in the state.
Central government — BharatNet Initiative: Aims to provide high-speed internet services to all gram panchayats in the country by 2025.
Uttar Pradesh government — Digital Infrastructure: Expects to complete the project within the next 12 months.

2026-03-10.title: India should partner with Japan in its mobility, clean energy journey: NITI Aayog member
2026-03-10.title_hi: भारत को अपनी गतिशीलता और स्वच्छ ऊर्जा यात्रा में जापान के साथ साझेदारी करनी चाहिए: नीति आयोग के सदस्य
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-10.context: India and Japan have complementary strengths in the future of mobility, according to V.K. Saraswat, a member of NITI Aayog. This partnership can help India in its transition to clean energy and advanced mobility solutions. The collaboration can also facilitate the exchange of technologies and expertise between the two countries. India's mobility sector is expected to grow significantly in the coming years, with a focus on electric vehicles and renewable energy. The partnership with Japan can provide India with the necessary support and guidance to achieve its clean energy goals.
key_points: NITI Aayog — Clean Energy Initiative: Partnering with Japan to achieve clean energy goals.
India — Mobility Sector: Expected to grow significantly in the coming years with a focus on electric vehicles.
Japan — Technology Exchange: Can provide India with the necessary support and guidance to achieve its clean energy goals.
India-Japan — Collaboration: Can facilitate the exchange of technologies and expertise between the two countries.
NITI Aayog — Advanced Mobility Solutions: Aims to promote the adoption of advanced mobility solutions in India.

2026-03-10.title: केन्द्रीय गृह एवं सहकारिता मंत्री श्री अमित शाह ने CISF के स्थापना दिवस पर उसके कर्मियों को शुभकामनाएं दी
2026-03-10.title_hi: केन्द्रीय औद्योगिक सुरक्षा बल (सीआईएसएफ) का स्थापना दिवस
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS3 — Internal Security: Paramilitary Forces and Their Roles
2026-03-10.context: The Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) is celebrating its Raising Day. The CISF was established in 1969 under an Act of Parliament. The force has been entrusted with the responsibility of providing security to various industrial establishments and government infrastructure. The CISF has a total strength of over 1.80 lakh personnel. The force is headed by a Director-General, who is an Indian Police Service (IPS) officer.
key_points: MHA — CISF: Established in 1969 with 3 battalions.
MHA — CISF: Currently has a strength of over 1.80 lakh personnel.
MHA — CISF: Provides security to various industrial establishments and government infrastructure.
MHA — CISF: Headed by a Director-General, who is an IPS officer.
MHA — CISF: Celebrating its Raising Day on 10 March.

2026-03-10.title: केन्द्रीय गृह एवं सहकारिता मंत्री श्री अमित शाह ने सावित्रीबाई फुले जी को उनकी पुण्यतिथि पर नमन किया
2026-03-10.title_hi: केन्द्रीय गृह एवं सहकारिता मंत्री ने सावित्रीबाई फुले जी को उनकी पुण्यतिथि पर नमन किया
2026-03-10.context: The Minister remembered Savitribai Phule's contributions to the field of education and social reform. She was a pioneer in the education of women and the marginalized sections of society. The Minister highlighted her efforts in promoting girls' education and empowering women. Savitribai Phule's legacy continues to inspire people across the country. The government has implemented various schemes to promote education and empowerment of women, including the Beti Bachao Beti Padhao scheme.
key_points: Ministry of Home Affairs — Tribute: Paid tribute to Savitribai Phule on her death anniversary.
Ministry of Education — Beti Bachao Beti Padhao: Implemented scheme to promote education and empowerment of women.
Government of India — Social Reform: Continues to work towards promoting social reform and education.
Ministry of Women and Child Development — Women Empowerment: Works towards empowering women and promoting their education.
National Commission for Women — Women's Education: Promotes education and empowerment of women.
